creator: kennedy.greg@gmail.com
text:
I actually really dislike the idea of "use arc4random UNLESS srand has been called", because when people want rand() to be random, they've historically been trained to called srand(time(0)) first.  So the outcome is backwards from what you'd expect: the pattern of srand(time(0)) -> rand() now becomes LESS random, when the dev was trying to get MORE random.

Meanwhile, people who didn't call srand() at all are presumably expecting stable output, and defaulting a new seed with unpredictable qualities now gives them UNstable, high-quality PRNG output with each run.

It's completely backwards.
